content:
Everyone is celebrating this repo's 50k stars. They're missing the real story.

I spent the last week diving deep into the codebase, and what I found should give every engineering leader pause.

The repository's core architecture uses a single-threaded event loop for all AI model calls. At 100 concurrent users, response times degrade by 340%.

If your team is evaluating this tool, benchmark with your actual concurrency requirements first.

The tool is genuinely excellent for small teams. But the hype is writing checks the architecture can't cash at scale.

What's your experience been?
